points.text: Practice good personal hygiene, including regular hand washing with soap and water.
Wear a face mask in public and when in close contact with others.
Maintain physical or social distancing to reduce transmission risk.
Ensure proper ventilation of indoor spaces to decrease virus spread.
Stay home if diagnosed with COVID-19 or if you believe you may be infected.
Cover coughs and sneezes with a tissue or your elbow to prevent spread.
Avoid sharing personal household items to reduce infection risk.
Stay up to date on COVID-19 vaccinations to enhance protection.
Keep unwashed hands away from your face to prevent virus entry.
Quarantine if exposed to someone with COVID-19 to prevent spread.
Stay up to date on your COVID-19 vaccine to prevent serious illness.
Ensure good indoor airflow to help prevent the spread of the virus.
Practice physical distancing to reduce the risk of transmission.
Wear a mask in appropriate settings to protect yourself and others.
Wash your hands often with soap and water for at least 20 seconds.
Use an alcohol-based hand sanitizer with at least 60% alcohol when needed.
Cover your coughs and sneezes with a tissue or your elbow.
Avoid close contact with anyone who is sick or has symptoms.
Stay home if you feel ill or have COVID-19 symptoms.
Test for COVID-19 if you have symptoms or after exposure.
Avoid crowded places and practice physical distancing of at least 1 metre from others.
Wear a mask in public places where community transmission is occurring.
Wash your hands frequently with soap and water or an alcohol-based hand rub.
Keep indoor spaces well ventilated to reduce virus transmission.
Seek medical care early if experiencing symptoms of COVID-19.
Stay informed about local COVID-19 guidance and updates from health authorities.
Get vaccinated to help reduce the spread of the virus.
Avoid going to public places if you or your child has symptoms.
Regularly clean and disinfect surfaces you frequently touch.
